steps.title: Day 6: Multi-Step Workflows
steps.body:
Single prompts are powerful. But the real magic happens when you chain multiple AI steps together — where the output of one step becomes the input for the next.

This is how AI operators build systems that handle complex work: research → analyze → draft → refine. Each step narrows and improves the result.

Today you'll build your first multi-step workflow.

A workflow is a chain of prompts where each step feeds the next. The pattern: INPUT → STEP 1 (process) → STEP 2 (refine) → STEP 3 (produce) → OUTPUT.

Think of it like an assembly line. Each station adds value. By the end, you have something no single prompt could produce.

steps.title: The Content Workflow
steps.body:
You're a marketing manager who needs to publish a thought leadership article every week. Instead of spending 4 hours writing each one, you build a 3-step workflow:

Step 1: Research — AI scans trending topics in your industry and suggests 5 angles
Step 2: Outline — AI creates a detailed outline with key points and supporting data
Step 3: Draft — AI writes the full article following your outline and style guide

Each step's output becomes the next step's input. Total time: 30 minutes instead of 4 hours.

steps.question: What makes a multi-step workflow more powerful than a single prompt?
options: You can review and adjust between steps, and each step specializes
Multi-step workflows are faster because AI processes in parallel
Single prompts can only do one thing
feedback: Token count isn't the point — quality and control are.
Correct. Between each step, you can check the output, adjust the next prompt, and ensure quality. Each step does one thing well.
AI processes steps sequentially in a workflow, not in parallel. The benefit is quality, not speed.
Single prompts can do multiple things — but they're harder to control and harder to get consistent results.

pairs.right: Create the first version of output

steps.question: Compare these two approaches: (1) One massive prompt asking for a complete article, vs (2) A 3-step workflow: research → outline → draft. Which produces better results and why?

questions.question: In a workflow, what happens between steps?
options: You review and adjust before proceeding
The AI decides whether to continue

questions.question: What's the minimum number of steps in a workflow?
True or false: Workflows are only useful for writing tasks.

fields.label: Step 2: What happens next?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Create a detailed outline with key points

fields.label: Step 3: Final output
fields.placeholder: e.g. Write the full piece following the outline

questions.prompt: Design a 3-step workflow for a task you do regularly. What happens at each step? Where do you review and adjust?
questions.placeholder:
Task: ...
Step 1: ...
Check: ...
Step 2: ...
Check: ...
Step 3: ...

steps.title: Day 6 Complete!
steps.body: You've built your first multi-step workflow. This is how professionals scale — by building systems, not one-off prompts. Tomorrow: quality control and testing your workflows.
